 {"id":"bd-2v0f","title":"Add gate issue type to beads","description":"Add 'gate' as a new issue type for async coordination.

- Add 'gate' to IssueType enum in internal/types/types.go
- Update validation to accept gate type
- Update CLI help text and completion

## Gate Type Semantics
- Gates are ephemeral (live in wisp storage)
- Managed by Deacon patrol
- Have special fields: await_type, await_id, timeout, waiters[]","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:31.331897-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:47:06.287781-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T11:47:06.287781-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2v0f","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:52.659005-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3","title":"Ephemeral issue cleanup and history compaction","description":"## Problem

Beads history grows without bound. Every message, handoff, work assignment
stays in issues.jsonl forever. Enterprise users will balk at "git as database."

## Solution: Two-Tier Cleanup

### Tier 1: Ephemeral Cleanup (v1)

bd cleanup --ephemeral --closed

- Deletes closed issues where ephemeral=true from issues.jsonl
- Safe: only removes explicitly marked ephemeral + closed
- Preserves git history (commits still exist)
- Run after swarm completion

### Tier 2: History Compaction (v2)

bd compact --squash

- Rewrites issues.jsonl to remove tombstones
- Optionally squashes git history (interactive rebase equivalent)
- Preserves Merkle proofs for deleted items
- Advanced: cold storage tiering

## HOP Context

| Layer | HOP Role | Persistence |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| Execution trace | None | Ephemeral |
| Work scaffolding | None | Summarizable |
| Work outcome | CV entry | Permanent |
| Validation record | Stake proof | Permanent |

"Execution is ephemeral. Outcomes are permanent. You can't squash your CV."

## Success Criteria

- After cleanup --ephemeral: issues.jsonl only contains persistent work
- Work outcomes preserved (CV entries)
- Validation records preserved (stake proofs)
- Execution scaffolding removed (transient coordination)","notes":"## Implementation Plan (REVISED after code review)

See history/EPHEMERAL_MOLECULES_DESIGN.md for comprehensive design + review.

## Key Simplification

After code review, Tier 1 is MUCH simpler than originally designed:

- **Original**: Separate ephemeral repo with routing.ephemeral config
- **Revised**: Just set Wisp: true in cloneSubgraph()

The wisp field and bd cleanup --wisp already exist\!

## Child Tasks (in dependency order)

1. **bd-2vh3.2**: Tier 1 - Ephemeral spawning (SIMPLIFIED) [READY]
   - Just add Wisp: true to template.go:474
   - Add --persistent flag to opt out
2. **bd-2vh3.3**: Tier 2 - Basic bd mol squash command
3. **bd-2vh3.4**: Tier 3 - AI-powered squash summarization
4. **bd-2vh3.5**: Tier 4 - Auto-squash on molecule completion
5. **bd-2vh3.6**: Tier 5 - JSONL archive rotation (DEFERRED: post-1.0)

## What Already Exists

| Component | Location |
|-----------|----------|
| Ephemeral field | internal/types/types.go:45 |
| bd cleanup --wisp | cmd/bd/cleanup.go:72 |
| cloneSubgraph() | cmd/bd/template.go:456 |
| loadTemplateSubgraph() | cmd/bd/template.go |

## HOP Alignment

'Execution is ephemeral. Outcomes are permanent. You can't squash your CV.'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:02:20.101367-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:50:02.958155-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:50:02.958155-08:00"}
